goal: Automated end-effector changeover at a tool stand: a UR10 starts with a parallel-jaw gripper mounted at its wrist, navigates to a 2-dock tool stand, releases the parallel-jaw into Dock_1 (by removing the wrist↔tool FixedJoint), repositions the wrist over Dock_2, and engages the vacuum gripper (by creating a new wrist↔tool FixedJoint). Tools are modeled as labelled Cube proxies (parallel_jaw and vacuum_cup) — geometry is intentionally minimal so the canonical exercises the swap *mechanism* rather than tool fidelity. Pattern_hint is 'other' because the success criterion is structural (which tool is parented to the wrist via FixedJoint at the end) rather than a workpiece-delivery criterion. First tool-changer canonical — establishes the shape for industrial-tool-changer-swap-001.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-tool-changer-swap (first tool-changer canonical):

1) Pattern_hint = 'other' — tool-swap is infrastructure, not a positive-motion canonical with workpiece delivery. Success is verified by inspecting which tool prim is parented to the wrist via FixedJoint at end-of-sequence (vacuum_cup present, parallel_jaw absent), NOT by checking a workpiece position. This is exactly the long-tail case the schema's pattern_hint='other' bucket is meant to absorb (canonical_schema.py §VALID_PATTERN_HINTS).

2) Topology — single station: UR10 base on 0.75m table at world origin; tool stand to the right at x=+0.55 with two docks separated along Y (Dock_1 at y=+0.10, Dock_2 at y=-0.10, both at z=0.85 atop the stand plate). Parallel-jaw tool starts mounted at the UR10 wrist (i.e. at the wrist's world pose); vacuum_cup tool starts parked in Dock_2. UR10 reach to stand is ~0.55m, well inside the 1.3m envelope.

3) Tool-stand modelling — /World/ToolStand is an Xform at [0.55, 0.0, 0.80] containing:
   • /World/ToolStand/Plate — flat Cube platform (scale 0.10×0.30×0.025)
   • /World/ToolStand/Dock_1 — visual receptacle Cube at [0.55, +0.10, 0.84] (scale 0.04×0.04×0.005); collision-only, no rigid body. Serves as the parked-tool position.
   • /World/ToolStand/Dock_2 — visual receptacle Cube at [0.55, -0.10, 0.84]; same shape as Dock_1.
   These dock prims are visual/collision proxies — they do NOT model real auto-tool-changer mechanical latches. The 'engagement' is purely a USD FixedJoint between wrist and tool prim, not a kinematic latch.

4) Tool prims (Cube proxies):
   • /World/Tools/ParallelJaw — Cube at the wrist position (initial). Scale 0.05×0.08×0.04; PhysicsRigidBodyAPI + PhysicsCollisionAPI + PhysicsMassAPI; primvars:displayColor=blue (visual distinction).
   • /World/Tools/VacuumCup — Cube parked in Dock_2 at [0.55, -0.10, 0.86]. Same physics apis; primvars:displayColor=orange.
   Tools live under /World/Tools/, NOT under the robot — the parent-child wrist relationship is established by FixedJoint, not USD hierarchy. This is the standard pattern used elsewhere for grasp-attach (cf. CP-30 FixedJoint at grip_close, CP-69 raycast→FixedJoint workaround).

5) FixedJoint engagement model:
   • Initial state: /World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_ParallelJaw — FixedJoint between /World/UR10/tool0 (wrist link, end-effector anchor on UR10) and /World/Tools/ParallelJaw. This is the 'currently mounted' tool.
   • Disengage step: re-author the FixedJoint to point at an unused anchor (or use a sentinel non-existent body1) — we model release by simply not creating the joint in the new state, and by physically moving the parallel-jaw prim into Dock_1 via set_attribute(xformOp:translate). USD-side: leave the original FixedJoint prim in place but break its semantic role; runtime release can also be modeled by setting joint:state:linear:enabled = False, but for the canonical-build phase we represent the END state (after swap completes), where:
     - /World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_ParallelJaw is REMOVED (no longer exists / is set inactive)
     - /World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_VacuumCup EXISTS as a FixedJoint between /World/UR10/tool0 and /World/Tools/VacuumCup
   This matches the verify_args.expected_state field which lists the post-swap relationships.

6) Tool-changer 'sequence' represented as code phases:
   PHASE 0: Build scene — UR10 at home, stand placed, both tool prims placed (parallel_jaw at wrist, vacuum_cup in Dock_2), initial FixedJoint Wrist_to_ParallelJaw active.
   PHASE 1: Approach dock 1 — move_to_pose to a pre-release pose above Dock_1 (so the parallel-jaw bottom hovers over the dock receptacle). target_position = [0.55, +0.10, 0.95] (10 cm above dock top).
   PHASE 2: Release parallel-jaw — set_attribute on FixedJoint to disable it (or destroy and recreate the tool prim's xformOp:translate to the dock position), then teleport-park /World/Tools/ParallelJaw to [0.55, +0.10, 0.86] via set_attribute(xformOp:translate).
   PHASE 3: Reposition over dock 2 — move_to_pose to [0.55, -0.10, 0.95] (above vacuum_cup in Dock_2).
   PHASE 4: Engage vacuum — create a NEW FixedJoint /World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_VacuumCup with body0=/World/UR10/tool0, body1=/World/Tools/VacuumCup. Move the vacuum-cup prim up to the wrist (via xformOp:translate to wrist position) to model the engagement. From this point on, vacuum follows wrist.

7) Failure modes (4 critical):
   F1: docking position drift — if the move_to_pose pre-release pose is more than ~5mm off the dock center, the released tool falls under gravity and ends up off-target (or rolls off the stand). Mitigation: pass tight target_position [0.55, +0.10, 0.95] and check with get_world_transform that the wrist actually arrived before issuing release.
   F2: FixedJoint not properly removed — if the old Wrist_to_ParallelJaw joint is not removed/disabled, both joints exist simultaneously and PhysX over-constrains the wrist (visible as the wrist locking up or both tools dragging behind it). Mitigation: explicit set_attribute(.../Wrist_to_ParallelJaw, 'physics:jointEnabled', False) before creating the new joint, and list_relationships to verify no two FixedJoints share the wrist body0.
   F3: Wrist-to-tool alignment too imprecise — vacuum_cup is placed at Dock_2 [0.55, -0.10, 0.86]; if the wrist is at [0.55, -0.10, 0.95] (9cm above), creating the FixedJoint without first moving the tool to the wrist results in a 9cm rigid offset baked into the joint. The tool then floats below the wrist. Mitigation: BEFORE create_articulated_joint, teleport the tool to the wrist position via set_attribute(xformOp:translate) so the joint's resting frame is zero-offset.
   F4: Simultaneous-tool race — if the engage step (create new FixedJoint) is issued in the same tool-call batch as the release step (disable old FixedJoint), and the executor runs them out of order, both joints exist at once. Mitigation: enforce sequential ordering via sim_control(action='step', step_count=2) between release and engage; OR insert get_world_transform call as a synchronization barrier.

8) Structural tags:
   • isaac:industry.tool_changer — new tag for tool-changer canonicals (this is the first; future tool-changer variants extend this cluster).
   • isaac:gesture.eoat_swap — gesture-axis tag for end-of-arm-tooling swap motion.
   • isaac:infra.tool_stand — infrastructure tag for the 2-dock tool stand.
   • isaac:robot.ur10 + isaac:robot.fixed_base.arm — robot lineage.
   • isaac:topology.single_station — single-cell layout.
   • isaac:pattern.fixed_joint_swap — swap mechanism is FixedJoint reattachment.

9) Yrkesroll-axis: the tool-changer is INFRASTRUCTURE not a human-role canonical, so no operator_role block (unlike CP-NEW-yrkesroll-machinist-cnc-load). The role-trio (roles/role_defaults/code_template) is still authored because this is a T1 canonical, but operator_role is omitted.

10) Motion-controllers — uses move_to_pose, which is in MOTION_PLANNING_TOOL_PREFIXES. Declare motion_controllers={verified:[], untested:[rmpflow, curobo], failed:{}} so the WARN for missing motion-controllers tag is satisfied.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")

# ── Work table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.8, 0.8, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU deterministic for FixedJoint reattach experiments ───────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── UR10 robot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="ur10",
    dest_path="/World/UR10",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Tool stand (Xform with plate + 2 docks) ──────────────────────────────────
# Plate at x=+0.55, two docks separated along Y (Dock_1 at +0.10, Dock_2 at -0.10).
create_prim(prim_path="/World/ToolStand", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Plate",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.55, 0.0, 0.80],
    scale=[0.05, 0.15, 0.0125],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Plate", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Plate", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.35, 0.35, 0.40]])

# Dock_1 — parking position for the parallel-jaw tool after release.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Dock_1",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.55, 0.10, 0.84],
    scale=[0.02, 0.02, 0.0025],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Dock_1", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Dock_1", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.2, 0.6, 0.9]])  # blue = parallel-jaw dock

# Dock_2 — parking position for the vacuum tool (initial state holds it here).
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Dock_2",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.55, -0.10, 0.84],
    scale=[0.02, 0.02, 0.0025],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Dock_2", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/ToolStand/Dock_2", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.9, 0.5, 0.2]])  # orange = vacuum-cup dock

# ── Tool prims (Cube proxies) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Both live under /World/Tools/ — the wrist parent-child relationship is
# expressed via FixedJoint (PhysX), NOT via USD hierarchy.
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Tools", prim_type="Xform")

# Parallel-jaw tool — starts mounted at the UR10 wrist (tool0 frame is ~1.20m up).
# Approximate wrist world pose with UR10 in home pose: [0.50, 0.0, 1.20] (a
# reasonable Z above the base; cuRobo/RMPflow handle the exact transform at runtime).
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Tools/ParallelJaw",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.50, 0.0, 1.20],
    scale=[0.025, 0.04, 0.02],
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Tools/ParallelJaw", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Tools/ParallelJaw", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.30)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Tools/ParallelJaw", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.2, 0.6, 0.9]])  # blue

# Vacuum-cup tool — starts parked in Dock_2.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Tools/VacuumCup",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.55, -0.10, 0.86],
    scale=[0.025, 0.025, 0.02],
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Tools/VacuumCup", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Tools/VacuumCup", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.30)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Tools/VacuumCup", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.9, 0.5, 0.2]])  # orange

# ── Initial FixedJoint: wrist ↔ parallel-jaw (tool is currently mounted) ─────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/ToolJoints", prim_type="Xform")
create_articulated_joint(
    joint_path="/World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_ParallelJaw",
    body0_path="/World/UR10/tool0",
    body1_path="/World/Tools/ParallelJaw",
    joint_type="fixed",
)

# ── PHASE 1: approach Dock_1 (pre-release pose above the dock) ───────────────
# Wrist moves to [0.55, +0.10, 0.95] — 9cm above Dock_1 top — so the parallel-jaw
# hangs directly above the dock receptacle ready for release.
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    target_position=[0.55, 0.10, 0.95],
    target_orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="ur10",
)
# Synchronization barrier — confirm wrist actually arrived before issuing release.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/UR10/tool0")

# ── PHASE 2: release parallel-jaw into Dock_1 ────────────────────────────────
# (a) Disable the wrist↔parallel-jaw FixedJoint so PhysX no longer constrains them.
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_ParallelJaw",
    attr_name="physics:jointEnabled",
    value=False,
)
# (b) Teleport the parallel-jaw prim to its parked pose in Dock_1.
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/Tools/ParallelJaw",
    attr_name="xformOp:translate",
    value=[0.55, 0.10, 0.86],
)

# ── PHASE 3: reposition wrist over Dock_2 ────────────────────────────────────
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    target_position=[0.55, -0.10, 0.95],
    target_orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="ur10",
)
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/UR10/tool0")

# ── PHASE 4: engage vacuum-cup tool ──────────────────────────────────────────
# (a) Teleport vacuum-cup to the wrist position FIRST (F3 mitigation — avoids
# baking a 9cm rigid offset into the FixedJoint resting frame).
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/Tools/VacuumCup",
    attr_name="xformOp:translate",
    value=[0.55, -0.10, 0.95],
)
# (b) Create the new wrist↔vacuum FixedJoint. From here on, vacuum follows wrist.
create_articulated_joint(
    joint_path="/World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_VacuumCup",
    body0_path="/World/UR10/tool0",
    body1_path="/World/Tools/VacuumCup",
    joint_type="fixed",
)

# ── Verification: post-swap state inspection ─────────────────────────────────
# Expected end-state:
#   • /World/Tools/ParallelJaw at ~[0.55, +0.10, 0.86] (parked in Dock_1)
#   • /World/Tools/VacuumCup at ~[0.55, -0.10, 0.95] (mounted on wrist)
#   • Wrist_to_ParallelJaw exists but physics:jointEnabled=False
#   • Wrist_to_VacuumCup exists and is the active engagement
prim_exists(prim_path="/World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_VacuumCup")
prim_exists(prim_path="/World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_ParallelJaw")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Tools/ParallelJaw")
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Tools/VacuumCup")
list_relationships(prim_path="/World/ToolJoints/Wrist_to_VacuumCup")
failure_modes: Docking position drift — if move_to_pose pre-release pose is more than ~5mm off Dock_1 center, the released parallel-jaw tool falls under gravity and lands off-target (or rolls off the stand). The tool is teleported to the dock pose via set_attribute(xformOp:translate) in PHASE 2, which masks the drift visually, but a real auto-tool-changer would rely on the wrist's accuracy. Mitigation: insert get_world_transform(/World/UR10/tool0) as a synchronization barrier and check the wrist xy is within 0.005m of [0.55, +0.10] before issuing release; if the planner fell short, retry move_to_pose with a tighter tolerance.

FixedJoint not properly removed — if the old Wrist_to_ParallelJaw FixedJoint is not disabled before the new Wrist_to_VacuumCup is created, BOTH joints constrain the wrist simultaneously. PhysX over-constrains: wrist locks up or both tools drag behind it. Mitigation: explicit set_attribute(.../Wrist_to_ParallelJaw, 'physics:jointEnabled', False) is issued in PHASE 2 step (a) BEFORE PHASE 4 creates the new joint; list_relationships at the end is a verification probe — exactly one of the two ToolJoints should be observed as active (jointEnabled=True).

Wrist-to-tool alignment too imprecise — when creating the new FixedJoint, PhysX bakes the CURRENT relative pose of body0 and body1 into the joint's resting frame. If the vacuum-cup is still at Dock_2 [0.55, -0.10, 0.86] when the wrist is at [0.55, -0.10, 0.95] (9 cm above), the joint encodes a 9-cm rigid offset and the tool floats below the wrist for the rest of the sim. Mitigation: PHASE 4 step (a) explicitly teleports VacuumCup to the wrist position [0.55, -0.10, 0.95] via set_attribute(xformOp:translate) BEFORE create_articulated_joint, guaranteeing a zero-offset rest frame.

Simultaneous-tool race — if the executor schedules PHASE 2 (release) and PHASE 4 (engage) tool calls in parallel rather than sequentially, both FixedJoints are alive at once during the gap. Mitigation: PHASE 3 (move_to_pose + get_world_transform) is interposed between release and engage, acting as a synchronization barrier; the get_world_transform call forces the executor to await PhysX state before issuing the next set_attribute or create_articulated_joint call. Additionally, sim_control(action='step', step_count=2) can be inserted between PHASE 2 and PHASE 4 if the executor's barrier behaviour is uncertain.

FixedJoint body0=/World/UR10/tool0 assumes the UR10 articulation actually exposes a 'tool0' link prim. robot_wizard for UR10 typically creates the standard UR10 link hierarchy with tool0 as the wrist-flange frame, but if a future Isaac Sim Kit version changes the URDF naming to 'flange' or 'ee_link' the create_articulated_joint will silently bind body0 to a non-existent prim and the joint will be a no-op. Mitigation: prim_exists(/World/UR10/tool0) after robot_wizard returns; if absent, fall back to /World/UR10/ee_link or /World/UR10/wrist_3_link. (UR10 standard URDF: tool0 is the wrist-flange frame; ee_link is its alias in some descriptions.)

Disabled-joint semantics may vary by PhysX version — physics:jointEnabled=False is the canonical way to disable an existing joint at runtime in USDPhysics schema, but older Isaac Sim Kit (pre-2024.1) used an attribute-overrides approach where jointEnabled defaulted to True with no override. If physics:jointEnabled is not respected (joint stays active), an alternative is to teleport body1 (the parallel-jaw) far away to break the natural rest pose, OR to author the joint prim with set_attribute(active, False). Detection: after PHASE 2, get_world_transform on /World/Tools/ParallelJaw — if the parallel-jaw position lags the wrist when the wrist moves to Dock_2 in PHASE 3, the joint is still active.
code_template:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.8, 0.8, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

create_prim(prim_path="/World/ToolStand", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path={{tool_stand_plate.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{tool_stand_plate.position}}, scale={{tool_stand_plate.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{tool_stand_plate.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(prim_path={{dock_1.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{dock_1.position}}, scale={{dock_1.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{dock_1.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(prim_path={{dock_2.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{dock_2.position}}, scale={{dock_2.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{dock_2.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

create_prim(prim_path="/World/Tools", prim_type="Xform")

create_prim(prim_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{tool_parallel_jaw.initial_position}}, scale={{tool_parallel_jaw.scale}})
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{tool_parallel_jaw.mass}})

create_prim(prim_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{tool_vacuum_cup.initial_position}}, scale={{tool_vacuum_cup.scale}})
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.path}}, schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{tool_vacuum_cup.mass}})

create_prim(prim_path="/World/ToolJoints", prim_type="Xform")
create_articulated_joint(
    joint_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.initial_joint_path}},
    body0_path={{primary_robot.wrist_link}},
    body1_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.path}},
    joint_type="fixed",
)

move_to_pose(
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_position=[0.55, 0.10, 0.95],
    target_orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="ur10",
)
get_world_transform(prim_path={{primary_robot.wrist_link}})

set_attribute(prim_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.initial_joint_path}}, attr_name="physics:jointEnabled", value=False)
set_attribute(prim_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.path}}, attr_name="xformOp:translate", value={{tool_parallel_jaw.parked_position}})

move_to_pose(
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    target_position=[0.55, -0.10, 0.95],
    target_orientation=[1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
    planner="rmpflow",
    robot_type="ur10",
)
get_world_transform(prim_path={{primary_robot.wrist_link}})

set_attribute(prim_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.path}}, attr_name="xformOp:translate", value={{tool_vacuum_cup.mounted_position}})
create_articulated_joint(
    joint_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.engage_joint_path}},
    body0_path={{primary_robot.wrist_link}},
    body1_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.path}},
    joint_type="fixed",
)

prim_exists(prim_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.engage_joint_path}})
prim_exists(prim_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.initial_joint_path}})
get_world_transform(prim_path={{tool_parallel_jaw.path}})
get_world_transform(prim_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.path}})
list_relationships(prim_path={{tool_vacuum_cup.engage_joint_path}})
extension_notes: First tool-changer canonical — establishes the swap-via-FixedJoint pattern (parallel_jaw → vacuum_cup) at a 2-dock tool stand. Pattern_hint='other' because the success criterion is structural (post-swap FixedJoint identity), not workpiece-delivery. Reuses the FixedJoint reattach pattern already verified in CP-30 (grip_close FixedJoint) and CP-69 (raycast→FixedJoint), applied at the wrist-to-EE seam rather than the EE-to-workpiece seam. Backlog notes that real auto-tool-changer hardware uses surface_gripper / setup_pick_place_controller for engagement, but for the first canonical we deliberately model the mechanism explicitly with create_articulated_joint to make the swap topology legible. A follow-up canonical (industrial-tool-changer-pneumatic-001 or similar) can layer surface_gripper on top once this baseline lands.
